title: Habeas adapter
description: Declarative definition of how to extract a user's own data from one service, in the user's live session. DATA, not code — no functions, no eval, no remote JS. This schema mirrors the runtime shape consumed by extension/src/runtime/inventory.js and enforced by extension/src/adapters/validate.js.

service.description: Short service slug used for sink folder/service naming.

version.description: Source definition version — a date YYYY-MM-DD (or semver). Shown in the catalog; the extension offers an update when the catalog version is newer than the installed one.

minVersion.description: Minimum Habeas extension version required to run this source (e.g. it uses a newer runtime feature). The catalog gates install and the extension skips it if too old. Absent = any version.

changelog.description: Per-version change notes (newest first), shown in the catalog details and as 'what's new' on update.

domain.description: Registrable domain (eTLD+1) all hosts share, e.g. carrefour.es. If omitted it is derived from api.host.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the service (e.g. ES, FR, US) or 'global'. Used to filter the catalog.

crossDomainHosts.description: Explicit allowlist of EXTRA hosts/domains outside `domain` that this source reads from or replays the session to (e.g. a bank whose API is on another domain). Presence requires an off-site consent screen. No wildcards.

currency.description: ISO currency for normalized records; defaults to EUR.

categories.description: The categories this source can emit (used for sink compatibility). Must be from the canonical catalog — see docs/categories.md.

streams.description: A source can expose several STREAMS (data sets) and, per stream, several FORMATS (artifact representations sharing the stream's list). A selectable output is a (stream, format) pair; each merges over the base (auth/api.host/match/domain), overriding list/schema/fields/categories (stream) and the artifact (format). When present, the base needs no list/schema/fields — each resolved output is validated instead.

field.description: Source field (dotted path allowed) whose value selects a category.

match.description: URL match patterns for the login site where the session is captured.

mode.description: bearer = replay the captured JWT; cookie = rely on the browser's cookies (credentials:include). Endpoints may mix (e.g. cookie list + bearer PDF).

tokenMatch.description: Regex the captured Authorization must match to be kept as a real user token. Default eyJ (a JWT); set a different pattern for an opaque bearer token, e.g. "^Bearer [0-9a-f-]{20,}$". Applied only to the source's own (incl. crossDomainHosts) API hosts.

context.description: Values to capture from a request URL in the live session (e.g. a DNI in /posicionGlobal/es/{DNI}) and store like auth (storage.session, never disk), then reference in api templates as {ctx.<name>} (groups.path, list.body, pdf). Never a hardcoded value — captured per user.

from.description: Where to read the value from (currently only 'url', the default).

match.description: Regex with exactly ONE capture group; group 1 is stored as the value.

resetCookies.description: When a tab is opened for a logged-out session, wipe the site's cookies first — recovers from a site that corrupts its own cookies and 500s the login (e.g. WiZink). Needs the `cookies` permission.

loginUrl.description: Sign-in page to open when no session is available (e.g. https://www.wizink.es/login). Defaults to the site root derived from match/api.host.

replayHeaders.description: Captured request headers to replay when calling the API (may be empty for cookie auth).

captureHosts.description: Extra hosts (besides api.host and match) whose requests are observed to capture auth. All must resolve to `domain` or a crossDomainHosts entry.

csrf.description: CSRF prelude: GET a page and regex-extract a single-use token, injected as {csrf} into list/groups/pdf bodies & paths. Refetched before each document download (tokens are often single-use).

path.description: Page whose HTML carries the token.

host.description: Host if different from api.host (same-domain guard applies).

match.description: Regex whose capture group 1 is the token.

groups.description: Optional parent enumeration (e.g. bank accounts): list the groups first, then `list` runs once per group with {group.*} templated into its path/params/referer. Each record carries its group (doc._group).

host.description: Host if different from api.host (same-domain guard still applies).

itemsPath.description: Dotted path to the groups array in the JSON response (or embedded state when from:html).

from.description: 'html' → read the groups out of the page's embedded SSR state at itemsPath.

body.description: [POST] Body template; {csrf} and any static params are substituted.

fields.description: Map group fields (id/name/iban/mask/currency…) from dotted paths, {group.*} templates, or (with rows) captured row fields; referenced as {group.<name>} and returned by the listGroups hook.

mask.description: Group field names to mask before exposing to a consumer (e.g. ['iban'] → ES12 **** 3456).

from.description: 'html' → the list is server-rendered: fetch the page and parse items from embedded JSON (at itemsPath) or the largest HTML table (rows → objects keyed by column header, plus `href`).

paging.description: Pagination strategy. Defaults to offsets if offsetsPath present, else cursor if nextPath present, else none. 'years' partitions the listing by year (see years).

years.description: [years] Year-partitioned listing (e.g. Amazon /your-orders): scan a bounded window of years, each with optional startIndex sub-paging. Dedupes across years by internalId.

format.description: Year value template; {y} → the year (default year-{y}).

back.description: Years back from the current year to scan (default 6).

startParam.description: Optional within-year offset param for sub-paging (e.g. startIndex).

stopAfterEmpty.description: Stop after this many CONSECUTIVE empty years (default 2) — adapts to the account's real history instead of a fixed back.

itemsPath.description: Dotted path to the array of items in the response (e.g. data.items). For from:html, the path into embedded page JSON (omit to parse an HTML table).

offsetsPath.description: [offsets] Dotted path to the offsets object merged into the next query.

nextPath.description: [cursor] Dotted path to the next cursor in the response.

cursorParam.description: [cursor] Query param name for the cursor (default: cursor).

pageParam.description: [page] Query param name for the page number (default: page).

offsetParam.description: [offset] Query param name for the item offset (default: offset).

offsetStep.description: [offset] Offset increment per page (the page size).

from.description: Query param name for the window start.

to.description: Query param name for the window end.

format.description: iso = full ISO timestamp (default), date = YYYY-MM-DD.

window.description: Lookback window for the range, e.g. 3y.

maxAgeDays.description: Drop documents/periods older than N days — keeps a source from requesting statements past a bank extra-auth wall (e.g. WiZink SMS beyond ~90 days). For periods sources the cut is applied before fetching each statement.

params.description: Static query params. `count` also drives the page-paging stop condition.

headers.description: Static headers the SPA sends for the list (e.g. dkt-ecom-origin).

referer.description: Referer to send per request (templated with {page}/{from}/{offset}) — for endpoints that only honour the offset when the Referer reflects the page. Set via declarativeNetRequest.

body.description: [POST] Body template; {group.*}, {csrf}, the paging param ({page}/{pagina}) and static params are substituted.

maxPeriods.description: [periods] Cap on how many past statement periods to fetch (default 24).

periods.description: Multi-period list assembly (e.g. WiZink card movements): fetch the current (unbilled) period plus one fetch per past statement date, all parsed with the shared list.rows row parser and concatenated. Each sub-config POSTs to list.path (query = params, form body = body with {group.*}/{csrf} filled; past.body may template {period} = a statement date). Statements behind the site extra-auth wall are skipped.

current.description: The current unbilled period; all its movements come back in one response.

dates.description: Returns the past statement dates; needs its own `rows` to extract them (e.g. from callOperations('YYYY-MM-DD')).

past.description: One past statement's movements; body may template {period} = the statement date.

pdf.description: Per-document PDF. Either a `path` (relative to api.host, GET by default; some services POST the invoice data as the body) OR a `urlField` naming a list-item field that already holds the FULL absolute https:// URL of the file.

path.description: Document path templated with {internalId}, {csrf}, {ctx.*}, {group.*} and any {field.path} from the list item (e.g. {docId}, {fileName}). Omit when using urlField or resolve.

resolve.description: Two-step document lookup: a resolver page (e.g. Amazon's invoice popover) carries the real, opaque document URL. Fetch path, regex linkMatch (capture group 1) out of the response TEXT for the URL (relative → prefixed with api.host), enforce the same-domain guard, then GET it. A non-match = no document (metadata-only).

linkMatch.description: Regex whose capture group 1 is the document URL in the resolver page.

urlField.description: Dotted path to a list-item field holding the FULL absolute https:// document URL (e.g. CaixaBank's statement `Url`). Fetched verbatim; its host must be api.host/domain or a crossDomainHosts entry (asserted at fetch time).

ext.description: File extension of the downloaded document when not a PDF (e.g. xls, csv). Default pdf.

host.description: PDF host if different from api.host (still bound by the same-domain guard / crossDomainHosts).

base64Field.description: Dotted path to a field in the JSON response holding the document BASE64-encoded (e.g. IKEA's GraphQL `data.receipt.receiptPdf`) — decoded to a blob instead of reading the response as raw binary. A null/empty value means no document for that item (metadata-only fallback).

mime.description: MIME type for a base64Field document (default application/pdf).

headers.description: Static request headers; overrides the runtime defaults (e.g. set a specific Accept). The default Accept for a PDF is */*.

referer.description: Referer header to send (templated with {internalId}/{field.path}) — for services that gate the PDF behind its detail page. Set via declarativeNetRequest.

detail.description: Per-document JSON detail (e.g. a full order). Saved as <id>.json. Preferred over a POST-generated PDF. Use `from:'list'` when the list endpoint already returns each item's full data (no extra request).

from.description: 'list' → the document IS the already-listed item's JSON (no path/request needed).

fields.description: Declarative HTML → JSON: build the structured detail record from the page HTML (pages with no embedded JSON, e.g. Amazon order-details). Each key → an {re|attr|tag|sel} extractor (list-row vocabulary), optionally scoped with {region:"data-component-name"}. date/total/amount are normalized.

const.description: Constant fields merged into every detail record (e.g. currency).

as.description: 'render' → open the path in a background tab, let the SPA render + fetch its data in the user's session, then capture the FINAL DOM self-contained (cross-browser; the faithful document for client-rendered pages). 'html' → fetch the path as a page and store it self-contained (server-rendered). 'invoice' → render a clean printable invoice from the detail JSON. All save a printable .html.

waitFor.description: [render] CSS selector to wait for before capturing (robust). If absent, waits waitMs.

waitMs.description: [render] Fixed wait in ms before capturing (default 3500) when waitFor is not set.

host.description: Detail host if different from api.host (same-domain guard applies).

headers.description: Static headers the SPA sends for this endpoint (e.g. dkt-ecom-origin).

referer.description: Referer header to send (templated with {internalId}) — for endpoints that validate it. Set via declarativeNetRequest.

document.description: The PRESENTABLE artifact (the archival/print document), separate from `detail` (structured data). A source may have BOTH; a sink chooses via accepts.artifacts. `as:render` (client-rendered page → captured), `as:html` (server-rendered page), `as:invoice` (generated from the detail JSON), or a plain PDF (path + optional method/body). Saved as <id>.html or <id>.pdf.

as.description: How to produce the document (see detail.as). Absent → a plain PDF at `path`.

waitFor.description: [render] CSS selector to wait for before capturing.

host.description: Host if different from api.host (same-domain guard applies).

fields.description: Map of normalized field name -> source field (dotted path). Recognized keys depend on `schema`: receipt (total, storeName, storeAddress, type, source), invoice (total, issuer, issuerAddress, number, type, source), transaction (amount, description, counterparty, direction, type, source), investment (instrument, isin, units, price, amount, operation, type).

schema.description: Target normalized schema: receipt@1 | invoice@1 | transaction@1 | investment@1.

itemLabel.description: Fallback display label template when a list row has no store/issuer/etc (e.g. a source whose list encrypts everything but the id). Templated with {internalId} / {field.path}, e.g. "Pedido {internalId}".

period.description: One period fetch in a multi-period list (see api.list.periods).

body.description: [POST] Form body template; {group.*}, {csrf} and (for past) {period} are substituted.

rows.description: Parse items out of HTML (for from:html lists/groups). Either `each` (a global regex, one match per item) or `row` (a container-class marker splitting per-item blocks).

each.description: Global regex; each match is one item. fields.<k> = {group:N} takes capture group N.

row.description: Class marker splitting the HTML into per-item blocks; fields extract from each block.

section.description: Optional: split the HTML into sections by a marker (section.each) and merge section-level fields into every item found within each section — so items inherit a property of their container (e.g. each order item carries its shipment's return status, whether the shipment holds one item or many).

each.description: Regex marking each section boundary (e.g. a shipment header).

fields.description: Field name -> {re|attr|tag|sel} extractor, read from the section and merged into its items (item fields win on conflict).
Per-field extraction. each mode: {group:N}. row mode: {re} (regex, group 1) | {attr} | {tag} | {sel} (text of an element with that class).

require.description: Field names that must be non-empty; incomplete rows are dropped.
